steps.title: Compare voting ages
steps.content: The passage places two voting-age requirements side by side: Panama requires citizens to be 18, while the Isle of Man allows voting at 16. The transition should signal comparison.

steps.title: Reject sequence and cause
steps.content: Choice A implies a list that does not continue. Choice C would make the Isle of Man an example of Panama's rule, which it is not. Choice D invents a cause-and-effect relationship.

steps.title: Signal the comparison
steps.content: Choice B directly frames the second voting requirement as a comparison with Panama's requirement, highlighting the difference between 18 and 16. The answer is B.
